documentation:
# Ics.py : iCalendar for Humans

[Original repository](https://github.com/C4ptainCrunch/ics.py) (GitHub) -
[Bugtracker and issues](https://github.com/C4ptainCrunch/ics.py/issues) (GitHub) -
[PyPi package](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/ics/) (ics) -
[Documentation](http://icspy.readthedocs.org/) (Read The Docs).

```{image} https://img.shields.io/github/license/c4ptaincrunch/ics.py.svg
:alt: Apache 2 License
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/ics/
```

Ics.py is a pythonic and easy iCalendar library.
Its goals are to read and write ics data in a developer friendly way.

iCalendar is a widely-used and useful format but not user friendly.
Ics.py is there to give you the ability of creating and reading this
format without any knowledge of it.

It should be able to parse every calendar that respects the
[rfc5545](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545) and maybe some more…
It also outputs rfc compliant calendars.

iCalendar (file extension `.ics`) is used by Google Calendar,
Apple Calendar, Android and many more.

Ics.py is available for Python>=3.6 and is Apache2 Licensed.

## Quickstart

```bash
$ pip install ics
```

```python
from ics import Calendar, Event
c = Calendar()
e = Event()
e.name = "My cool event"
e.begin = '2014-01-01 00:00:00'
c.events.add(e)
c.events
# [<Event 'My cool event' begin:2014-01-01 00:00:00 end:2014-01-01 00:00:01>]
with open('my.ics', 'w') as my_file:
    my_file.writelines(c.serialize_iter())
# and it's done !
```

More examples are available in the
[documentation](http://icspy.readthedocs.org/).

## Documentation

All the [documentation](http://icspy.readthedocs.org/) is hosted on
[readthedocs.org](http://readthedocs.org/) and is updated automatically
at every commit.

- [Quickstart](http://icspy.readthedocs.org/)
- [API](http://icspy.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api.html)
- [About](http://icspy.readthedocs.org/en/latest/about.html)

## Contribute

Contribution are welcome of course! For more information, see
[contributing](https://github.com/C4ptainCrunch/ics.py/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.rst).

## Testing & Docs

```bash
# setup virtual environment
$ sudo pip install virtualenv
$ virtualenv ve
$ source ve/bin/activate

# tests
$ pip install -r requirements.txt
$ pip install -r dev/requirements-test.txt
$ python setup.py test

# tests coverage
$ pip install -r requirements.txt
$ pip install -r dev/requirements-test.txt
$ python setup.py test
$ coverage html
$ firefox htmlcov/index.html

# docs
$ pip install -r requirements.txt
$ pip install -r dev/requirements-doc.txt
$ cd doc
$ make html
```

## Links

- [rfc5545](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545)
- [Vulgarised RFC](http://www.kanzaki.com/docs/ical/)

```{image} http://i.imgur.com/KnSQg48.jpg
:align: center
:alt: Parse ALL the calendars!
:target: https://github.com/C4ptainCrunch/ics.py
```

classes.name: ics.icalendar.Calendar

methods.name: ics.icalendar.Calendar.__init__
documentation:
Instantiates a new Calendar.

Args:

: imports (**str**): data to be imported into the Calendar,
  events (**Set[Event]**): Events to be added to the calendar
  todos (Set[Todo]): Todos to be added to the calendar
  creator (string): uid of the creator program.

If `imports` is specified, every other argument will be ignored.

methods.name: ics.icalendar.Calendar.parse_multiple
documentation:
"
Parses an input string that may contain mutiple calendars
and retruns a list of {class}`ics.event.Calendar`

methods.name: ics.icalendar.Calendar.__iter__
documentation:
Returns:
iterable: an iterable version of seralize(), line per line
(with line-endings).

Example:
    Can be used to write calendar to a file:

    >>> c = Calendar(); c.events.add(Event(name="My cool event"))
    >>> open('my.ics', 'w').writelines(c)

methods.name: ics.icalendar.Calendar.creator
documentation:
Get or set the calendar's creator.

|  Will return a string.
|  May be set to a string.
|  Creator is the PRODID iCalendar property.
|  It uniquely identifies the program that created the calendar.

methods.name: ics.icalendar.Calendar.clone
documentation:
Returns:
    Calendar: an exact deep copy of self

Represents an unique rfc5545 iCalendar.

Attributes:

    events: a set of Event contained in the Calendar
    todos: a set of Todo contained in the Calendar
    timeline: a Timeline instance linked to this Calendar

functions.name: ics.utils.arrow_get
documentation:
this function exists because ICS uses ISO 8601 without dashes or
colons, i.e. not ISO 8601 at all.

functions.name: ics.utils.parse_duration
documentation: Return a timedelta object from a string in the DURATION property format

functions.name: ics.utils.timedelta_to_duration
documentation:
Return a string according to the DURATION property format
from a timedelta object

classes.name: ics.todo.Todo

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.__init__
documentation:
Instantiates a new {class}`ics.todo.Todo`.

Args:

: uid (string): must be unique
  dtstamp (Arrow-compatible)
  completed (Arrow-compatible)
  created (Arrow-compatible)
  description (string)
  begin (Arrow-compatible)
  location (string)
  percent (int): 0-100
  priority (int): 0-9
  name (string) : rfc5545 SUMMARY property
  url (string)
  due (Arrow-compatible)
  duration (datetime.timedelta)
  alarms ({class}`ics.alarm.Alarm`)
  status (string)

Raises:

: ValueError: if `duration` and `due` are specified at the same time

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.begin
documentation:
Get or set the beginning of the todo.

Will return an 

{class}`Arrow`

 object.

May be set to anything that 

{func}`Arrow.get`

 understands.

If a due time is defined (not a duration), .begin must not
be set to a superior value.

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.due
documentation:
Get or set the end of the todo.

Will return an 

{class}`Arrow`

 object.

May be set to anything that 

{func}`Arrow.get`

 understands.

If set to a non null value, removes any already
existing duration.

Setting to None will have unexpected behavior if
begin is not None.

Must not be set to an inferior value than self.begin.

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.duration
documentation:
Get or set the duration of the todo.

|  Will return a timedelta object.
|  May be set to anything that timedelta() understands.
|  May be set with a dict ({"days":2, "hours":6}).
|  If set to a non null value, removes any already
    existing end time.

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.__eq__
documentation: Two todos are considered equal if they have the same uid.

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.__ne__
documentation: Two todos are considered not equal if they do not have the same uid.

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.clone
documentation:
Returns:
    Todo: an exact copy of self

methods.name: ics.todo.Todo.__hash__
documentation:
Returns:
    int: hash of self. Based on self.uid.

A todo list entry.

Can have a start time and duration, or start and due time,
or only start/due time.

methods.name: ics.component.Component.serialize
documentation: Returns the component in an iCalendar format.

methods.name: ics.component.Component.serialize_iter
documentation:
Returns the component in an iCalendar format.

This returns an Iterable of multiple string chunks which should be concatenated to form the actual ics representation.
Note that individual items of the returned Iterable not necessarily correspond to individual lines,
linebreaks are contained at the right places within the items.

methods.name: ics.component.Component.__str__
documentation: Starting from version 0.9, returns a short description of the Component.

classes.name: ics.grammar.parse.ContentLine

methods.name: ics.grammar.parse.ContentLine.parse
documentation: Parse a single iCalendar-formatted line into a ContentLine

methods.name: ics.grammar.parse.ContentLine.clone
documentation: Makes a copy of itself

Represents one property line.

For example:

`FOO;BAR=1:YOLO` is represented by

`ContentLine('FOO', {'BAR': ['1']}, 'YOLO'))`

Args:

> name: The name of the property (uppercased for consistency and
>
> : easier comparison)
>
> params: A map name:list of values
> value: The value of the property

documentation: Makes a copy of itself

Represents an iCalendar object.
Contains a list of ContentLines or Containers.

Args:

    name: the name of the object (VCALENDAR, VEVENT etc.)
    items: Containers or ContentLines

classes.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm

methods.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm.__init__
documentation:
Instantiates a new {class}`ics.alarm.BaseAlarm`.

Adheres to RFC5545 VALARM standard: <http://icalendar.org/iCalendar-RFC-5545/3-6-6-alarm-component.html>

Args:

: trigger (datetime.timedelta OR datetime.datetime) : Timespan to alert before parent action, or absolute time to alert
  repeat (integer) : How many times to repeat the alarm
  duration (datetime.timedelta) : Duration between repeats

Raises:

: ValueError: If trigger, repeat, or duration do not match the RFC spec.

methods.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm.trigger
documentation:
The trigger condition for the alarm

| Returns either a timedelta or datetime object

methods.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm.repeat
documentation:
Number of times to repeat alarm

| Returns an integer for number of alarm repeats
| Value must be >= 0

methods.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm.duration
documentation:
Duration between alarm repeats

| Returns a timedelta object
| Timespan must return positive total_seconds() value

methods.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm.action
documentation: VALARM action to be implemented by concrete classes

methods.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm.__eq__
documentation: Two alarms are considered equal if they have the same type and base values.

methods.name: ics.alarm.base.BaseAlarm.clone
documentation:
Returns:
    Alarm: an exact copy of self
A calendar event VALARM base class

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.__init__
documentation:
Instanciates a new Timeline.
(You should not have to instanciate a new timeline by yourself)

Args:

: calendar : {class}`ics.icalendar.Calendar`

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.__iter__
documentation:
Iterates on every event from the {class}`ics.icalendar.Calendar` in chronological order

Note :
: - chronological order is defined by the comparaison operators in {class}`ics.event.Event`
  - Events with no `begin` will not appear here. (To list all events in a `Calendar` use `Calendar.events`)

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.included
documentation:
Iterates (in chronological order) over every event that is included
in the timespan between `start` and `stop`

Args:
    start : (Arrow object)
    stop : (Arrow object)

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.overlapping
documentation:
Iterates (in chronological order) over every event that has an intersection
with the timespan between `start` and `stop`

Args:
    start : (Arrow object)
    stop : (Arrow object)

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.start_after
documentation:
Iterates (in chronological order) on every event from the {class}`ics.icalendar.Calendar` in chronological order.
The first event of the iteration has a starting date greater (later) than `instant`

Args:

: instant : (Arrow object) starting point of the iteration

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.at
documentation:
Iterates (in chronological order) over all events that are occuring during `instant`.

Args:
    instant (Arrow object)

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.on
documentation:
Iterates (in chronological order) over all events that occurs on `day`

Args:
    day (Arrow object)
    strict (bool): if True events will be returned only if they are            strictly *included* in `day`.

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.today
documentation:
Iterates (in chronological order) over all events that occurs today

Args:
    strict (bool): if True events will be returned only if they are            strictly *included* in `day`.

methods.name: ics.timeline.Timeline.now
documentation: Iterates (in chronological order) over all events that occurs now

classes.name: ics.event.Event

methods.name: ics.event.Event.__init__
documentation:
Instantiates a new {class}`ics.event.Event`.

Args:

: name: rfc5545 SUMMARY property
  begin (Arrow-compatible)
  end (Arrow-compatible)
  duration
  uid: must be unique
  description
  created (Arrow-compatible)
  last_modified (Arrow-compatible)
  location
  url
  transparent
  alarms
  attendees
  categories
  status
  organizer
  classification

Raises:

: ValueError: if `end` and `duration` are specified at the same time

methods.name: ics.event.Event.has_end
documentation:
Return:
    bool: self has an end

methods.name: ics.event.Event.add_attendee
documentation: Add an attendee to the attendees set

methods.name: ics.event.Event.begin
documentation:
Get or set the beginning of the event.

Will return an 

{class}`Arrow`

 object.

May be set to anything that 

{func}`Arrow.get`

 understands.

If an end is defined (not a duration), .begin must not
be set to a superior value.

methods.name: ics.event.Event.end
documentation:
Get or set the end of the event.

Will return an 

{class}`Arrow`

 object.

May be set to anything that 

{func}`Arrow.get`

 understands.

If set to a non null value, removes any already
existing duration.

Setting to None will have unexpected behavior if
begin is not None.

Must not be set to an inferior value than self.begin.

methods.name: ics.event.Event.duration
documentation:
Get or set the duration of the event.

|  Will return a timedelta object.
|  May be set to anything that timedelta() understands.
|  May be set with a dict ({"days":2, "hours":6}).
|  If set to a non null value, removes any already
    existing end time.

methods.name: ics.event.Event.geo
documentation:
Get or set the geo position of the event.

|  Will return a namedtuple object.
|  May be set to any Geo, tuple or dict with latitude and longitude keys.
|  If set to a non null value, removes any already
    existing geo.

methods.name: ics.event.Event.all_day
documentation:
Return:
    bool: self is an all-day event

methods.name: ics.event.Event.make_all_day
documentation:
Transforms self to an all-day event.

The event will span all the days from the begin to the end day.

methods.name: ics.event.Event.join
documentation:
Create a new event which covers the time range of two intersecting events

All extra parameters are passed to the Event constructor.

Args:
    other: the other event

Returns:
    a new Event instance

methods.name: ics.event.Event.clone
documentation:
Returns:
    Event: an exact copy of self

methods.name: ics.event.Event.__hash__
documentation:
Returns:
    int: hash of self. Based on self.uid.

A calendar event.

Can be full-day or between two instants.
Can be defined by a beginning instant and
a duration *or* end instant.

Unsupported event attributes can be found in `event.extra`,
a {class}`ics.parse.Container`. You may add some by appending a
{class}`ics.parse.ContentLine` to `.extra`
